preview_text:
The model favors Saudi Arabia to win at 40%, with Cape Verde at 28% and a draw at 31%. The underdog enjoys meaningful but not overwhelming chances, trailing by roughly 12 percentage points in the outright market.

The draw is the model's second-most likely outcome, indicating competitive balance. The win probabilities spread across all three outcomes without extreme concentration, suggesting neither team is heavily favored to control the match decisively. A draw remains a plausible result in a moderately even contest.

The market consensus diverges from the model. Sportsbooks assign Cape Verde a notably higher win probability of 38%, a gap of roughly 10 percentage points. The books also rate the draw lower at 27% and compress Saudi Arabia's advantage to 35%. Polymarket pricing falls between the two sources. The dashboard does not adjudicate which estimate is more accurate; the divergence reflects genuine disagreement on match likelihood.
